question: How many orbital launches will SpaceX conduct between July 1 and July 23, 2026?

factors.description: Empirical ~0.45/day cadence over remaining 12 days adds ~5.4 (mean)
Multiple scheduled mid-late July missions (Starlink, SDA Tranche 1) support sustained cadence

summary: SpaceX orbital launches July 1-23, 2026. Five launches are already locked in through July 11 (July 2, 5, 7, 9, 11 — all Falcon 9). The remaining 12 days (July 12-23) should add roughly Poisson/negative-binomial-distributed launches at SpaceX's empirical cadence of ~0.45/day (2025: 0.452/day; 2026 YTD: 0.424/day), giving a mean of ~5.4 more. Total centers on ~10.4, median 10. A Monte Carlo simulation with rate uncertainty (0.40-0.52/day) and overdispersion (accounting for scrubs and manifest shifts) yields P10=7, P50=10, P90=14. Starship Flight 13 is listed as suborbital and would not count. The distribution sits comfortably within the open bounds of 5.5-20.5.
reasoning:
My forecast is complete.

**Summary:** SpaceX orbital launches July 1-23, 2026 — median **10**, with an 80% interval of roughly **7 to 14**.

The key insight is that 5 launches are already locked in through July 11, and the only real uncertainty is the remaining 12-day window (July 12-23). Applying SpaceX's steady empirical cadence of ~0.45 launches/day (consistent across 2025 full-year, 2026 YTD, and July-to-date) to those remaining days adds a mean of ~5.4 more, for a total centered on ~10.4. I ran a negative-binomial Monte Carlo to account for scrubs, weather, and manifest variance, producing well-calibrated tails (P10=7, P90=14) that sit comfortably within the question's open bounds of 5.5-20.5. Starship Flight 13 is listed as suborbital and would not count toward the total.
